comment_text.sn15.5:1.3: Indian cosmology assumes a cyclic universe, where an “eon” is the time for an entire universe to come into existence, evolve, decay, then disappear.

root_text.sn15.5:1.2: Atha kho aññataro bhikkhu yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavantaṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ nisīdi. Ekamantaṁ nisinno kho so bhikkhu bhagavantaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.sn15.5:1.5: So na sukaro saṅkhātuṁ ettakāni vassāni iti vā, ettakāni vassasatāni iti vā, ettakāni vassasahassāni iti vā, ettakāni vassasatasahassāni iti vā”ti.
root_text.sn15.5:2.3: “Seyyathāpi, bhikkhu, mahāselo pabbato yojanaṁ āyāmena yojanaṁ vitthārena yojanaṁ ubbedhena acchinno asusiro ekagghano.
root_text.sn15.5:2.4: Tamenaṁ puriso vassasatassa vassasatassa accayena kāsikena vatthena sakiṁ sakiṁ parimajjeyya.
root_text.sn15.5:2.5: Khippataraṁ kho so, bhikkhu, mahāselo pabbato iminā upakkamena parikkhayaṁ pariyādānaṁ gaccheyya, na tveva kappo.
root_text.sn15.5:2.7: Evaṁ dīghānaṁ kho, bhikkhu, kappānaṁ neko kappo saṁsito, nekaṁ kappasataṁ saṁsitaṁ, nekaṁ kappasahassaṁ saṁsitaṁ, nekaṁ kappasatasahassaṁ saṁsitaṁ.
root_text.sn15.5:2.11: yāvañcidaṁ, bhikkhu, alameva sabbasaṅkhāresu nibbindituṁ, alaṁ virajjituṁ, alaṁ vimuccitun”ti.

translation_text.sn15.5:1.2: Then a mendicant went up to the Buddha, bowed, sat down to one side, and asked him,
translation_text.sn15.5:1.5: It’s not easy to calculate how many years, how many hundreds or thousands or hundreds of thousands of years it lasts.”
translation_text.sn15.5:2.1: “But sir, is it possible to give a simile?”
translation_text.sn15.5:2.3: “Suppose there was a huge stone mountain, a league long, a league wide, and a league high, with no cracks or holes, one solid mass.
translation_text.sn15.5:2.4: And as each century passed someone would stroke it once with a fine cloth from Kāsi.
translation_text.sn15.5:2.5: By this means the huge stone mountain would be worn away before the eon comes to an end.
translation_text.sn15.5:2.6: That’s how long an eon is.
translation_text.sn15.5:2.7: And we’ve transmigrated through many such eons, many hundreds, many thousands, many hundreds of thousands.
translation_text.sn15.5:2.9: This transmigration has no known beginning. …
translation_text.sn15.5:2.11: This is quite enough for you to become disillusioned, dispassionate, and freed regarding all conditions.”
